pending.body: finding_summary: `iterative_minimal` resume recovery still breaks for successful terminal iterations when the parsed sidecar is missing but the backend's raw transcript is not plain JSON. In [src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6321), `recover_iterative_payload_from_raw_output` reads the `.raw` file and blindly `serde_json::from_str`s it ([6328-6351]). But successful invocations are allowed to have `parsed_payload != raw_output`: [src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:334) returns the already-parsed payload without looking at the raw text when `parsed_payload` is non-null, and [src/adapters/process_backend.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/adapters/process_backend.rs:199) extracts/parses structured output separately while persisting full `stdout_text` as the raw artifact ([288-290]). The new code also explicitly treats parsed-sidecar persistence as best-effort and keeps running anyway ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6260)); if a run then crashes after the terminal iteration but before stage commit, resume hard-fails instead of recovering ([6572-6586]). That leaves `iterative_minimal` runs unrecoverable in exactly the sidecar-loss scenario this change is trying to support for real process backends whose raw output is not pure JSON.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 14 -> Plan and Implement
